## Key Metadata: Quantitative PCR (qPCR)

[Targeted species detection — additional fields vs metabarcoding]{style="color:#E8A020; font-style:italic;"}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {style="color:white; padding:12px; border-radius:8px; margin-bottom:8px;"}
⛵ **Field Sampling**

- Date, Coordinates, Environmental measurements
:::

::: {style="color:white; padding:12px; border-radius:8px;"}
🧪 **PCR Protocol**

- PCR conditions

:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {style="color:white; padding:12px; border-radius:8px; margin-bottom:8px;"}
🧬 **DNA Extraction**

- Concentration, Extraction method, Extracted amount
:::

::: {style="color:white; padding:12px; border-radius:8px;"}
🎯 **Target & Quantity**

- Target gene region, primers
- Copy number, LOD, Cq, baseline

:::
:::

::::

::: {style="font-size:0.65em; color:#5A8FA8; text-align:center; margin-top:6px;"}
*Category 3 — Targeted species detection · `basisOfRecord = MaterialSample`*
:::

::: notes
qPCR = Category 3 — targeted species detection. Adds qPCR-specific fields: probeReporter, probeQuencher, estimatedNumberOfCopies, thresholdQuantificationCycle, and baselineValue. Key difference from metabarcoding: you are detecting one specific taxon, so scientificName and scientificNameID are required from the start. Use basisOfRecord = MaterialSample.
:::
